verses.latin_vulgate: Loquentibus autem illis ad populum, supervenerunt sacerdotes, et magistratus templi, et sadducæi,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λαλούντων δὲ αὐτῶν πρὸς τὸν λαὸν ἐπέστησαν αὐτοῖς οἱ ἱερεῖς καὶ ὁ στρατηγὸς τοῦ ἱεροῦ καὶ οἱ σαδδουκαῖοι
verses.king_james: And as they spake unto the people, the priests, and the captain of the temple, and the Sadducees, came upon them,

verses.latin_vulgate: dolentes quod docerent populum, et annuntiarent in Jesu resurrectionem ex mortuis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διαπονούμενοι διὰ τὸ διδάσκειν αὐτοὺς τὸν λαὸν καὶ καταγγέλλειν ἐν τῷ ἰησοῦ τὴν ἀνάστασιν τὴν ἐκ νεκρῶν
verses.king_james: Being grieved that they taught the people, and preached through Jesus the resurrection from the dead.

verses.latin_vulgate: et injecerunt in eos manus, et posuerunt eos in custodiam in crastinum: erat enim jam vespera.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐπέβαλον αὐτοῖς τὰς χεῖρας καὶ ἔθεντο εἰς τήρησιν εἰς τὴν αὔριον ἦν γὰρ ἑσπέρα ἤδη
verses.king_james: And they laid hands on them, and put *them* in hold unto the next day: for it was now eventide.

verses.latin_vulgate: Multi autem eorum qui audierant verbum, crediderunt: et factus est numerus virorum quinque millia.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πολλοὶ δὲ τῶν ἀκουσάντων τὸν λόγον ἐπίστευσαν καὶ ἐγενήθη ὁ ἀριθμὸς τῶν ἀνδρῶν ὡσεὶ χιλιάδες πέντε
verses.king_james: Howbeit many of them which heard the word believed; and the number of the men was about five thousand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Factum est autem in crastinum, ut congregarentur principes eorum, et seniores, et scribæ, in Jerusalem:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγένετο δὲ ἐπὶ τὴν αὔριον συναχθῆναι αὐτῶν τοὺς ἄρχοντας καὶ πρεσβυτέρους καὶ γραμματεῖς
verses.king_james: And it came to pass on the morrow, that their rulers, and elders, and scribes,

verses.latin_vulgate: et Annas princeps sacerdotum, et Caiphas, et Joannes, et Alexander, et quotquot erant de genere sacerdotali.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἴς ἰερουσαλήμ καὶ ἄνναν τὸν ἀρχιερέα καὶ καϊάφαν, καὶ ἰωάννην καὶ ἀλέξανδρον καὶ ὅσοι ἦσαν ἐκ γένους ἀρχιερατικοῦ
verses.king_james: And Annas the high priest, and Caiaphas, and John, and Alexander, and as many as were of the kindred of the high priest, were gathered together at Jerusalem.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et statuentes eos in medio, interrogabant: In qua virtute, aut in quo nomine fecistis hoc vos?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ στήσαντες αὐτοὺς ἐν τῷ μέσῳ ἐπυνθάνοντο ἐν ποίᾳ δυνάμει ἢ ἐν ποίῳ ὀνόματι ἐποιήσατε τοῦτο ὑμεῖς
verses.king_james: And when they had set them in the midst, they asked, By what power, or by what name, have ye done this?

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc repletus Spiritu Sancto Petrus, dixit ad eos: Principes populi, et seniores, audite:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε πέτρος πλησθεὶς πνεύματος ἁγίου εἶπεν πρὸς αὐτούς ἄρχοντες τοῦ λαοῦ καὶ πρεσβύτεροι τοῦ ἰσραὴλ,
verses.king_james: Then Peter, filled with the Holy Ghost, said unto them, Ye rulers of the people, and elders of Israel,

verses.latin_vulgate: si nos hodie dijudicamur in benefacto hominis infirmi, in quo iste salvus factus est,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ ἡμεῖς σήμερον ἀνακρινόμεθα ἐπὶ εὐεργεσίᾳ ἀνθρώπου ἀσθενοῦς ἐν τίνι οὗτος σέσωσται
verses.king_james: If we this day be examined of the good deed done to the impotent man, by what means he is made whole;

verses.latin_vulgate: notum sit omnibus vobis, et omni plebi Israël, quia in nomine Domini nostri Jesu Christi Nazareni, quem vos crucifixistis, quem Deus suscitavit a mortuis, in hoc iste astat coram vobis sanus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: γνωστὸν ἔστω πᾶσιν ὑμῖν καὶ παντὶ τῷ λαῷ ἰσραὴλ ὅτι ἐν τῷ ὀνόματι ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ τοῦ ναζωραίου ὃν ὑμεῖς ἐσταυρώσατε ὃν ὁ θεὸς ἤγειρεν ἐκ νεκρῶν ἐν τούτῳ οὗτος παρέστηκεν ἐνώπιον ὑμῶν ὑγιής
verses.king_james: Be it known unto you all, and to all the people of Israel, that by the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, whom ye crucified, whom God raised from the dead, *even* by him doth this man stand here before you whole.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hic est lapis qui reprobatus est a vobis ædificantibus, qui factus est in caput anguli:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ λίθος ὁ ἐξουθενηθεὶς ὑφ᾽ ὑμῶν τῶν οἰκοδόμούντων, ὁ γενόμενος εἰς κεφαλὴν γωνίας
verses.king_james: This is the stone which was set at nought of you builders, which is become the head of the corner.

verses.latin_vulgate: et non est in alio aliquo salus. Nec enim aliud nomen est sub cælo datum hominibus, in quo oporteat nos salvos fieri.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οὐκ ἔστιν ἐν ἄλλῳ οὐδενὶ ἡ σωτηρία οὔτε γὰρ ὄνομά ἐστιν ἕτερον ὑπὸ τὸν οὐρανὸν τὸ δεδομένον ἐν ἀνθρώποις ἐν ᾧ δεῖ σωθῆναι ἡμᾶς
verses.king_james: Neither is there salvation in any other: for there is none other name under heaven given among men, whereby we must be saved.

verses.latin_vulgate: Videntes autem Petri constantiam, et Joannis, comperto quod homines essent sine litteris, et idiotæ, admirabantur, et cognoscebant eos quoniam cum Jesu fuerant:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: θεωροῦντες δὲ τὴν τοῦ πέτρου παῤῥησίαν καὶ ἰωάννου καὶ καταλαβόμενοι ὅτι ἄνθρωποι ἀγράμματοί εἰσιν καὶ ἰδιῶται ἐθαύμαζον ἐπεγίνωσκόν τε αὐτοὺς ὅτι σὺν τῷ ἰησοῦ ἦσαν
verses.king_james: Now when they saw the boldness of Peter and John, and perceived that they were unlearned and ignorant men, they marvelled; and they took knowledge of them, that they had been with Jesus.

verses.latin_vulgate: hominem quoque videntes stantem cum eis, qui curatus fuerat, nihil poterant contradicere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τόν δὲ ἄνθρωπον βλέποντες σὺν αὐτοῖς ἑστῶτα τὸν τεθεραπευμένον οὐδὲν εἶχον ἀντειπεῖν
verses.king_james: And beholding the man which was healed standing with them, they could say nothing against it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Jusserunt autem eos foras extra concilium secedere: et conferebant ad invicem,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κελεύσαντες δὲ αὐτοὺς ἔξω τοῦ συνεδρίου ἀπελθεῖν συνέβαλον πρὸς ἀλλήλους
verses.king_james: But when they had commanded them to go aside out of the council, they conferred among themselves,

verses.latin_vulgate: dicentes: Quid faciemus hominibus istis? quoniam quidem notum signum factum est per eos omnibus habitantibus Jerusalem: manifestum est, et non possumus negare.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγοντες τί ποιήσομεν τοῖς ἀνθρώποις τούτοις ὅτι μὲν γὰρ γνωστὸν σημεῖον γέγονεν δι᾽ αὐτῶν πᾶσιν τοῖς κατοικοῦσιν ἰερουσαλὴμ φανερόν καὶ οὐ δυνάμεθα ἀρνήσασθαι
verses.king_james: Saying, What shall we do to these men? for that indeed a notable miracle hath been done by them *is* manifest to all them that dwell in Jerusalem; and we cannot deny *it*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed ne amplius divulgetur in populum, comminemur eis ne ultra loquantur in nomine hoc ulli hominum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλ᾽ ἵνα μὴ ἐπὶ πλεῖον διανεμηθῇ εἰς τὸν λαόν ἀπειλῇ ἀπειλησώμεθα αὐτοῖς μηκέτι λαλεῖν ἐπὶ τῷ ὀνόματι τούτῳ μηδενὶ ἀνθρώπων
verses.king_james: But that it spread no further among the people, let us straitly threaten them, that they speak henceforth to no man in this name.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et vocantes eos, denuntiaverunt ne omnino loquerentur neque docerent in nomine Jesu.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ καλέσαντες αὐτοὺς παρήγγειλαν αὐτοῖς τὸ καθόλου μὴ φθέγγεσθαι μηδὲ διδάσκειν ἐπὶ τῷ ὀνόματι τοῦ ἰησοῦ
verses.king_james: And they called them, and commanded them not to speak at all nor teach in the name of Jesus.

verses.latin_vulgate: Petrus vero et Joannes respondentes, dixerunt ad eos: Si justum est in conspectu Dei vos potius audire quam Deum, judicate.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ πέτρος καὶ ἰωάννης ἀποκριθέντες πρὸς αὐτούς εἶπον εἰ δίκαιόν ἐστιν ἐνώπιον τοῦ θεοῦ ὑμῶν ἀκούειν μᾶλλον ἢ τοῦ θεοῦ κρίνατε
verses.king_james: But Peter and John answered and said unto them, Whether it be right in the sight of God to hearken unto you more than unto God, judge ye.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non enim possumus quæ vidimus et audivimus non loqui.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ δυνάμεθα γὰρ ἡμεῖς ἃ εἴδομεν καὶ ἠκούσαμεν μὴ λαλεῖν
verses.king_james: For we cannot but speak the things which we have seen and heard.

verses.latin_vulgate: At illi comminantes dimiserunt eos, non invenientes quomodo punirent eos propter populum: quia omnes clarificabant id quod factum fuerat in eo quod acciderat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ δὲ προσαπειλησάμενοι ἀπέλυσαν αὐτούς μηδὲν εὑρίσκοντες τὸ πῶς κολάσωνται αὐτούς διὰ τὸν λαόν ὅτι πάντες ἐδόξαζον τὸν θεὸν ἐπὶ τῷ γεγονότι
verses.king_james: So when they had further threatened them, they let them go, finding nothing how they might punish them, because of the people: for all *men* glorified God for that which was done.

verses.latin_vulgate: Annorum enim erat amplius quadraginta homo, in quo factum fuerat signum istud sanitatis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐτῶν γὰρ ἦν πλειόνων τεσσαράκοντα ὁ ἄνθρωπος ἐφ᾽ ὃν ἐγεγόνει τὸ σημεῖον τοῦτο τῆς ἰάσεως
verses.king_james: For the man was above forty years old, on whom this miracle of healing was shewed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dimissi autem venerunt ad suos, et annuntiaverunt eis quanta ad eos principes sacerdotum et seniores dixissent.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπολυθέντες δὲ ἦλθον πρὸς τοὺς ἰδίους καὶ ἀπήγγειλαν ὅσα πρὸς αὐτοὺς οἱ ἀρχιερεῖς καὶ οἱ πρεσβύτεροι εἶπον
verses.king_james: And being let go, they went to their own company, and reported all that the chief priests and elders had said unto them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui cum audissent, unanimiter levaverunt vocem ad Deum, et dixerunt: Domine, tu es qui fecisti cælum et terram, mare et omnia quæ in eis sunt:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ δὲ ἀκούσαντες ὁμοθυμαδὸν ἦραν φωνὴν πρὸς τὸν θεὸν καὶ εἶπον, δέσποτα σὺ ὁ θεὸς ὁ ποιήσας τὸν οὐρανὸν καὶ τὴν γῆν καὶ τὴν θάλασσαν καὶ πάντα τὰ ἐν αὐτοῖς
verses.king_james: And when they heard that, they lifted up their voice to God with one accord, and said, Lord, thou *art* God, which hast made heaven, and earth, and the sea, and all that in them is:

verses.latin_vulgate: qui Spiritu Sancto per os patris nostri David pueri tui dixisti: [Quare fremuerunt gentes, et populi meditati sunt inania?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ διὰ στόματος δαβὶδ τοῦ παιδός σου εἰπών ἱνατί ἐφρύαξαν ἔθνη καὶ λαοὶ ἐμελέτησαν κενά
verses.king_james: Who by the mouth of thy servant David hast said, Why did the heathen rage, and the people imagine vain things?

verses.latin_vulgate: Astiterunt reges terræ, et principes convenerunt in unum adversus Dominum, et adversus Christum ejus.]
verses.greek_textus_receptus: παρέστησαν οἱ βασιλεῖς τῆς γῆς καὶ οἱ ἄρχοντες συνήχθησαν ἐπὶ τὸ αὐτὸ κατὰ τοῦ κυρίου καὶ κατὰ τοῦ χριστοῦ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: The kings of the earth stood up, and the rulers were gathered together against the Lord, and against his Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Convenerunt enim vere in civitate ista adversus sanctum puerum tuum Jesum, quem unxisti, Herodes et Pontius Pilatus, cum gentibus et populis Israël,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: συνήχθησαν γὰρ ἐπ᾽ ἀληθείας ἐπὶ τὸν ἅγιον παῖδά σου ἰησοῦν ὃν ἔχρισας ἡρῴδης τε καὶ πόντιος πιλᾶτος σὺν ἔθνεσιν καὶ λαοῖς ἰσραήλ
verses.king_james: For of a truth against thy holy child Jesus, whom thou hast anointed, both Herod, and Pontius Pilate, with the Gentiles, and the people of Israel, were gathered together,

verses.latin_vulgate: facere quæ manus tua et consilium tuum decreverunt fieri.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ποιῆσαι ὅσα ἡ χείρ σου καὶ ἡ βουλή σου προώρισεν γενέσθαι
verses.king_james: For to do whatsoever thy hand and thy counsel determined before to be done.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et nunc, Domine, respice in minas eorum, et da servis tuis cum omni fiducia loqui verbum tuum,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ τὰ νῦν κύριε ἔπιδε ἐπὶ τὰς ἀπειλὰς αὐτῶν καὶ δὸς τοῖς δούλοις σου μετὰ παῤῥησίας πάσης λαλεῖν τὸν λόγον σου
verses.king_james: And now, Lord, behold their threatenings: and grant unto thy servants, that with all boldness they may speak thy word,

verses.latin_vulgate: in eo quod manum tuam extendas ad sanitates, et signa, et prodigia fieri per nomen sancti filii tui Jesu.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν τῷ τὴν χεῖρά σου ἐκτείνειν σε εἰς ἴασιν καὶ σημεῖα καὶ τέρατα γίνεσθαι διὰ τοῦ ὀνόματος τοῦ ἁγίου παιδός σου ἰησοῦ
verses.king_james: By stretching forth thine hand to heal; and that signs and wonders may be done by the name of thy holy child Jesus.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum orassent, motus est locus in quo erant congregati: et repleti sunt omnes Spiritu Sancto, et loquebantur verbum Dei cum fiducia.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ δεηθέντων αὐτῶν ἐσαλεύθη ὁ τόπος ἐν ᾧ ἦσαν συνηγμένοι καὶ ἐπλήσθησαν ἅπαντες πνεύματος ἁγίου καὶ ἐλάλουν τὸν λόγον τοῦ θεοῦ μετὰ παῤῥησίας
verses.king_james: And when they had prayed, the place was shaken where they were assembled together; and they were all filled with the Holy Ghost, and they spake the word of God with boldness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Multitudinis autem credentium erat cor unum et anima una: nec quisquam eorum quæ possidebat, aliquid suum esse dicebat, sed erant illis omnia communia.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦ δὲ πλήθους τῶν πιστευσάντων ἦν ἡ καρδία καὶ ἡ ψυχὴ μία καὶ οὐδὲ εἷς τι τῶν ὑπαρχόντων αὐτῷ ἔλεγεν ἴδιον εἶναι ἀλλ᾽ ἦν αὐτοῖς ἅπαντα κοινά
verses.king_james: And the multitude of them that believed were of one heart and of one soul: neither said any *of them* that ought of the things which he possessed was his own; but they had all things common.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et virtute magna reddebant Apostoli testimonium resurrectionis Jesu Christi Domini nostri: et gratia magna erat in omnibus illis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ μεγάλῃ δυνάμει ἀπεδίδουν τὸ μαρτύριον οἱ ἀπόστολοι τῆς ἀναστάσεως τοῦ κυρίου ἰησοῦ χάρις τε μεγάλη ἦν ἐπὶ πάντας αὐτούς
verses.king_james: And with great power gave the apostles witness of the resurrection of the Lord Jesus: and great grace was upon them all.

verses.latin_vulgate: Neque enim quisquam egens erat inter illos. Quotquot enim possessores agrorum aut domorum erant, vendentes afferebant pretia eorum quæ vendebant,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐδὲ γὰρ ἐνδεής τις ὑπῇρχεν ἐν αὐτοῖς ὅσοι γὰρ κτήτορες χωρίων ἢ οἰκιῶν ὑπῆρχον πωλοῦντες ἔφερον τὰς τιμὰς τῶν πιπρασκομένων
verses.king_james: Neither was there any among them that lacked: for as many as were possessors of lands or houses sold them, and brought the prices of the things that were sold,

verses.latin_vulgate: et ponebant ante pedes Apostolorum. Dividebatur autem singulis prout cuique opus erat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐτίθουν παρὰ τοὺς πόδας τῶν ἀποστόλων διεδίδοτο δὲ ἑκάστῳ καθότι ἄν τις χρείαν εἶχεν
verses.king_james: And laid *them* down at the apostles' feet: and distribution was made unto every man according as he had need.

verses.latin_vulgate: Joseph autem, qui cognominatus est Barnabas ab Apostolis (quod est interpretatum, Filius consolationis), Levites, Cyprius genere,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰωσῆς δὲ ὁ ἐπικληθεὶς βαρναβᾶς ὑπὸ τῶν ἀποστόλων ὅ ἐστιν μεθερμηνευόμενον υἱὸς παρακλήσεως λευίτης κύπριος τῷ γένει
verses.king_james: And Joses, who by the apostles was surnamed Barnabas, (which is, being interpreted, The son of consolation,) a Levite, *and* of the country of Cyprus,

verses.latin_vulgate: cum haberet agrum, vendidit eum, et attulit pretium, et posuit ante pedes Apostolorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑπάρχοντος αὐτῷ ἀγροῦ πωλήσας ἤνεγκεν τὸ χρῆμα καὶ ἔθηκεν παρά τοὺς πόδας τῶν ἀποστόλων
verses.king_james: Having land, sold *it*, and brought the money, and laid *it* at the apostles' feet.
